title: calytrix breviseta subsp. stipulosa
intro: Calytrix breviseta subsp. stipulosa, commonly known as the Stipulate Calytrix or Bush Pea, is a plant species native to Western Australia, belonging to the family Myrtaceae. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional Aboriginal medicine, where it has been used to treat various health conditions. The Stipulate Calytrix is said to have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which contribute to its health benefits, including wound healing, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and flowers are typically used in teas, infusions, and tinctures to harness its medicinal properties, although further research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and safety for widespread use.
